notes: KNOWN_FAILING (daemon=bug): gemini-cli records a mid-turn API failure as a type:"error" MessageRecord in ~/.gemini/tmp/<project>/chats/session-*.jsonl (PR #13300, merged 2025-11-19). The gemini-cli parser dispatches strictly on type — user/gemini handled, default (incl. error/info/warning) skipped (parser.go:100-109,63-65) — and infers turn_done only from a final text-only 'gemini' message (parser.go:208-212). So the error line is silently dropped, no turn_done fires, IsAgentDone() never trips, and with no inactivity sweep on the working state (stale-tool timer deleted in PR #106) the session STICKS in working. The spec below asserts the CORRECT contract (working→ready, never stuck); the live recording is expected to leave the session in working until the parser fix lands (map type:"error" to a terminal signal). Recorded via the INTERACTIVE driver (gemini -y under tmux: send + wait_turn + 12s trailing sleep), NOT headless gemini -p, so the process outlives the daemon's observation window. The mid-turn failure inducer is a record prerequisite (GOOGLE_GEMINI_BASE_URL is honored only on the API-key path, not default oauth-personal — issue #15430). The phase shape mirrors basic-turn's because the visible contract is the same arc.

text: Session appears in ready shortly after launch
Launcher PID bound by cwd shortly after launch
User prompt drives ready → working before the API error lands
Mid-turn API failure recorded as a type:"error" line should drive working → ready; state must not stick in working (KNOWN_FAILING today: parser skips type:"error" → no turn_done → stays working)
